name: Distributed SSH Brute Force with Credential Stuffing
description: A large-scale distributed SSH brute force and credential stuffing campaign targeting internet-facing Linux servers, modeled after real-world attacks documented by SANS Internet Storm Center and Shodan honeypot data. Attackers use compromised botnet nodes to rotate source IPs and evade rate limiting, followed by cryptocurrency miner deployment and lateral movement to internal database servers. This pattern matches campaigns attributed to TeamTNT and similar threat groups.

phases.name: Masscan Port Discovery
phases.description: Attacker uses Masscan and ZMap to rapidly scan large IP ranges for exposed SSH services (port 22) and alternative SSH ports (2222, 2200). Scanning originates from multiple botnet nodes to distribute the scan load and avoid IP-based blocking. This mirrors real TeamTNT reconnaissance patterns observed in Shodan data.

expected_logs.description: SYN packets from multiple source IPs to port 22
SYN scan pattern: high packet rate, no completed handshakes
Suricata alert: ET SCAN Potential SSH Scan
Connection attempts logged by sshd
Rate-limiting triggered on firewall for port 22

phases.name: Distributed SSH Credential Stuffing
phases.description: Attacker launches a distributed credential stuffing attack using leaked credential databases (from prior breaches like RockYou2021 and LinkedIn 2012 dump). The attack rotates through botnet IPs to stay under fail2ban thresholds (typically 5 attempts per IP). Hydra and custom Python scripts are used with a curated wordlist targeting common Linux service accounts.

expected_logs.description: Failed SSH login for root from 91.215.85.17
Failed SSH login attempts for multiple usernames
fail2ban jail triggered for SSH
PAM authentication failure for deploy account
Suricata alert: ET COMPROMISED Known hostile host

phases.name: Successful SSH Authentication
phases.description: Attacker successfully authenticates to the deploy service account using a reused password from a previous LinkedIn breach. The password 'Deploy2019!' was found in the credential stuffing database and matched the unchanged service account password.

expected_logs.description: Successful SSH login for deploy from 91.215.85.17 after 4500 failures
New session opened for user deploy
bash shell spawned for deploy user
SSH session established from Tor exit node IP

phases.name: Privilege Escalation via CVE-2021-4034 (PwnKit)
phases.description: Attacker escalates privileges from the deploy service account to root using CVE-2021-4034 (PwnKit), a local privilege escalation vulnerability in polkit's pkexec utility. The exploit is downloaded from the attacker's staging server and compiled on the target. This CVE was widely exploited in real-world TeamTNT campaigns throughout 2022-2023.

expected_logs.description: curl downloading exploit from 91.215.85.17:8080/pwnkit.c
gcc compilation of exploit binary
pkexec executed with unusual arguments (CVE-2021-4034)
UID change from 1001 (deploy) to 0 (root)
New root shell spawned from pkexec context

phases.name: Lateral Movement to Database Server
phases.description: With root access on the web server, the attacker discovers database credentials in application configuration files (wp-config.php, .env files) and SSH keys in /root/.ssh/. They use these to pivot to the internal database server. The attacker also deploys the Diamorphine rootkit for persistence on the web server.

expected_logs.description: grep -r password /var/www/ searching for credentials
Read access to /var/www/html/wp-config.php
SSH key-based login from srv-web-01 (10.0.1.50) to srv-db-01 (10.0.1.51)
Internal SSH connection from web server to database server
Diamorphine rootkit kernel module loaded via insmod

phases.name: Database Dump and Cryptominer Deployment
phases.description: Attacker dumps the entire PostgreSQL database containing user credentials and PII, then deploys XMRig cryptocurrency miner on both compromised servers. The database dump is exfiltrated to the attacker's infrastructure, while the cryptominer generates Monero revenue. This dual-purpose attack (data theft + cryptojacking) is a hallmark of TeamTNT operations.

expected_logs.description: pg_dumpall executed by root user
pg_dumpall command execution
Large outbound data transfer to 91.215.85.17
XMRig miner process started as hidden service
Stratum mining protocol connection to pool.minexmr.com:4444
expected_logs.event_id: IDS Alert (Suricata ET POLICY Cryptocurrency Miner)

expected_detection_points: Distributed port scan from multiple known-malicious IPs
SSH brute force pattern with IP rotation to evade fail2ban
Successful SSH login from known threat IP after brute force pattern
Credential harvesting from application configuration files
Unusual internal SSH lateral movement (web server to database)
PostgreSQL database dump and outbound exfiltration
XMRig cryptocurrency miner deployment and Stratum protocol traffic
